message.content:
{
  "analysis": "`/app/steal.py` has been created and successfully ran, saving `/app/stolen_A1.npy` with shape `(20, 10)`. The verification command was entered but its output is not visible in the terminal log, so I still need to verify that the recovered matrix matches true `A1` up to row permutation and nonzero scaling.",
  "plan": "Run a focused verification script that loads `/app/stolen_A1.npy`, normalizes both stolen and true `A1` rows, computes absolute cosine similarities, checks per-row max similarity, and uses linear-sum assignment to confirm a one-to-one matching with high similarity. If all matched similarities are >= 0.999, the task is complete.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import numpy as np
from forward import A1

stolen = np.load('/app/stolen_A1.npy', allow_pickle=False)
print('stolen shape:', stolen.shape)
print('finite:', bool(np.isfinite(stolen).all()))

tn = A1 / np.linalg.norm(A1, axis=1, keepdims=True)
sn = stolen / np.linalg.norm(stolen, axis=1, keepdims=True)
sims = np.abs(sn @ tn.T)

print('max similarity per recovered row:', sims.max(axis=1))
print('mean max similarity:', float(sims.max(axis=1).mean()))
print('min max similarity:', float(sims.max(axis=1).min()))
print('all rows matched >=0.999:', bool((sims.max(axis=1) >= 0.999).all()))

try:
    from scipy.optimize import linear_sum_assignment
    cost = -sims
    r, c = linear_sum_assignment(cost)
    matched = sims[r, c]
    print('assignment similarities:', matched)
    print('assignment all >=0.999:', bool((matched >= 0.999).all()))
except Exception as e:
    print('assignment check failed:', e)
PY
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}
